label: Large-scale pretraining

node.label: Large-scale pretraining
node.definition: Pretraining learns broad representations or generative behavior from large, diverse corpora before task-specific adaptation or deployment.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Large-scale pretraining is to connect Self-supervised learning, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy to downstream capabilities including Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning, Parametric memory, Large language models.
examples: substrate for Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning
limitations: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Self-supervised learning, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.

dependencies.rationale: Large-scale pretraining uses Self-supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Large-scale pretraining is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
Large-scale pretraining is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Autoregressive / next-step prediction.
Large-scale pretraining is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Masked prediction objectives.
Large-scale pretraining uses Data collection, curation & filtering as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Large-scale pretraining uses Empirical scaling laws as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Large-scale pretraining uses Learning-rate schedules, warmup & stabilization as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Large-scale pretraining uses Regularization, dropout & weight decay as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
